sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 38-year-old male, presented to the office complaining of low back pain that started after raking leaves in his yard 2 hours prior to arrival.
He reports that the pain is worse when standing and improves with rest and a hot shower.
He had a similar episode 3 years ago that resolved on its own.
He denies taking any medication for the pain, including ibuprofen, which he tried without relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of similar low back pain episode 3 years ago, but no other relevant medical history was mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in his right leg.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had some pain on flexion, pain on palpation around the L5 area, and normal dorsiflexion.
The pulses were equal in all extremities.
Test Results: X-ray of the low back was normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a probable acute low back sprain.
Problem list: Low back sprain; suspected radiculopathy; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally twice a day for pain and inflammation.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretching and exercises to improve the patient's condition.
I instructed the patient to rest and avoid strenuous activities for a few days.
I will re-evaluate the patient in two weeks if the pain does not improve, and we will consider an MRI at that time.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in two weeks to re-evaluate the pain and consider further imaging if necessary.
